items.title: New Clarity Act emerges that's a start on the final draft, makes ethics rule temporary
items.description: Lawmakers have circulated text of the latest draft that will be pushed toward a Senate finish line, but they're still debating a core government-ethics piece.

items.title: Revolut hits $115 billion valuation in employee share sale: WSJ
items.description: The company reported strong 2025 performance, with a $2.3 billion pre-tax profit, $6 billion in revenue, and over 75 million customers.

items.title: SEC's Peirce warns some DeFi vaults, onchain lending may fall under securities laws
items.description: The commissioner said onchain vaults and lending strategies could resemble investment funds or advisers, depending on how they are structured.

items.title: UK digital bond plans hinge on one missing piece: onchain cash
items.description: Experts say Britain's digital gilt pilot can unlock trapped liquidity in financial markets only if regulators approve an onchain stablecoin.

items.title: Crypto Long & Short: The crypto question isn't what to own — it's what you can survive holding
items.description: In this week's Crypto Long & Short, Lionsoul Global’s Gregory Mall writes that the primary crypto allocation decision is size, not selection.

items.title: U.S. seeks forfeiture of $25 million in crypto tied to romance and investment scams
items.description: The cases add to more than $800 million that authorities say a dedicated federal scam task force has recovered.

items.title: Forget Nvidia: The next big AI trade could be crypto and blockchain
items.description: Franklin Templeton’s Sandy Kaul and Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire argue that as autonomous AI agents start spending money on their own, blockchain networks will power the next big AI trade.

items.title: Kalshi rolls out Midterm Hubs ahead of the November elections in the U.S.
items.description: The prediction markets platform seeks to position itself as the primary source for election odds ahead of the midterm elections this year.

items.title: Digital Chamber sues Illinois officials over new state 0.2% crypto tax
items.description: The group alleged that the tax signed into law in June “discriminates against people who transact in digital assets“ and should be blocked from implementation and enforcement.

items.title: BitGo, OTC Markets plan tokenized securities access for broker-dealers
items.description: The proposed alliance would enable more than 150 broker-dealers to trade and settle digital asset securities through OTC Link ATS using BitGo’s custody infrastructure.

items.title: Bitcoin price avoids major Iran jitters as S&P 500 ‘short squeeze’ on horizon
items.description: Bitcoin and US stocks stayed strong in the face of US-Iran escalation as analysis predicted BTC price action outperforming equities later.

items.title: The digital euro: Surveillance money, or a better alternative to cash?
items.description: Proponents of the digital euro say its a better alternative to cash that’s fully backed by the ECB. But critics argue it could provide the EU with too much power over its citizens.

items.title: Franklin Templeton calls agentic AI next ‘killer’ use case for blockchain
items.description: Franklin Templeton’s digital assets lead said that the autonomous AI agent economy will increase demand for blockchain protocols hosting machine-to-machine micropayments.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Bitcoin analysis eyes ‘serious volume’ after Binance sees 9K BTC daily outflow
items.description: Binance set multimonth records for net BTC outflows on Tuesday, adding to an emerging trend of positive inflows to the US spot Bitcoin ETFs.

items.title: Zilliqa Ledger app vulnerability lets attackers recover signer’s private keys
items.description: A security vulnerability in the Zilliqa Ledger app is enabling attackers to reconstruct private keys using publicly available onchain data.

items.title: Houthis deploy missiles and drones to attack ships in southern Red Sea, naval group says
items.description: Attacks on ships transiting the Bab el-Mandeb threaten to cut off millions of barrels per day of Saudi crude oil exports.

items.title: OpenAI cyber models broke out of training environment to hack Hugging Face
items.description: The incident is unique because it was "driven, end to end, by an autonomous AI agent system," according to Hugging Face.

items.title: Senate crypto bill would ban federal officials — including presidents — from issuing digital assets
items.description: A new version of the Clarity Act would impose the first limits on federal officials sponsoring or issuing cryptocurrency.

items.title: Congressional stock trading curb due to get House vote amid public support for a ban
items.description: The House is scheduled to vote on Wednesday on a bill that would bar members of Congress from purchasing additional stocks while in office.

items.title: Under-the-radar beverage name captures trader's attention
items.description: Michael Khouw breaks down how he's trading this stock ahead of earnings.

items.title: Kevin Warsh has homed in on three key phrases. How Fed watchers interpret them
items.description: In five public appearances, Warsh has used the phrase "family fight" 13 times, returning to "first principles" 11 times and "inflation is a choice" for the Fed six times.

items.title: Mortgage rates are rising again, but homebuyers are seeing some advantages
items.description: Mortgage rates last week rose to the highest level since last August, but homebuyer demand climbed as well, as buyers see more supply.

items.title: $100M New Jersey deli fraudster James Patten sentenced to 21 months in prison
items.description: The notorious New Jersey deli fraud case led to losses of more than $5 million for victims that included two U.S. universities.

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xucMhqLOKeo! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: AMD plans to invest to up $5 billion into Anthropic as it seeks to cut into Nvidia’s dominance
items.description: The two companies also struck a chip deal.

items.title: Can you beat the market? Maybe with this strategy.
items.description: This could be the right time to buy a momentum fund

items.title: This is the ideal retirement strategy if you want to keep your healthcare costs down
items.description: Plus, more ways to lower how much you pay out of pocket for medical care in retirement.

items.title: Do I use $300,000 of my $1.2 million retirement savings so my daughter can attend her dream college?
items.description: “Our daughter is a highly ranked competitive softball pitcher with a 3.94 GPA.”

items.title: Global oil prices hold gains after topping $95 a barrel for the first time in 6 weeks as hopes dim for de-escalation of Iran war
items.description: Oil prices were holding on to their gains after climbing to a six-week high on Wednesday morning after the U.S. struck Iran for the 11th night in a row and President Donald Trump warned that the U.S. will bomb Iranian bridges and power plants if Tehran shoots at ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: My second husband and I have kids from previous relationships. Should my house go to him or my children if I die first?
items.description: “We live in a house that I purchased entirely with money I had before we were married.”

items.title: I will definitely claim Social Security early. Why do so few people talk about the elephant in the room?
items.description: “The strongest argument for claiming benefits earlier goes beyond the traditional break-even analyses.”

items.title: Fed’s favorite inflation tracker is getting an overhaul — just as the central bank weighs interest-rate hikes. What’s going on?
items.description: The inflation tracker that exerts the biggest influence on the actions of the Federal Reserve is getting a makeover just as a divided central bank weighs whether to raise U.S. interest rates this fall.

- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Trump Administration Plans to Stick With Tariffs, Trade Representative Says
items.description: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

items.title: Stocks and the Economy Are Increasingly Relying on the A.I. Boom
items.description: Investment in artificial intelligence and related companies is lifting the stock market and spending across the economy.

items.description: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

items.title: See How Houthis Put the Red Sea at Risk as an Alternative Oil Route
items.description: Saudi Arabia has diverted huge amounts of oil to the Red Sea since the Iran war began, but now the Iranian-backed Houthi militants say that they intend to block that route.

items.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
items.description: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.

items.title: How Donald Trump Jr.’s 1789 Capital Is Cashing In Without Apology
items.description: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

items.title: Trump Plans to Impose Tariffs on Generic Drugs
items.description: The administration has repeatedly said it will impose steep pharmaceutical tariffs but has not followed through on them.
